readme.text:
# Editframe Elements Repository

> 🚧 Do not expose or share to anybody outside of editframe without direct authorization

This repository contains several npm packages and associated libraries to support the editframe elements web components.

## Getting started

1. Install Docker. https://www.docker.com/products/docker-desktop/
2. Clone this repository

⚠️ If you have a

directory in the root of the repository from prior use of this
repository. Delete that directory before running start.

Run

to verify the docker images build and all tests pass.

## Development

Run

to run the test suite.

Run

to create docker containers.

### Dev Projects

Dev projects that will live update with changes to the source code can be found in:
. These will run when you run
. They run in a docker container and bind at [

, you'll need to connect VSCode to the devcontainer.

First: install the [devcontainers extension]( devcontainers extension).
Second: open this project's directory in VSCode.
Third: open the command pallet and run

The VSCode window will reload and connect to the running devcontainer.

All shell commands should be executed in the integrated VSCode terminal, (or in a terminal attached to the docker container.)

### VSCode Extensions

Install the [Biome extension](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=biomejs.biome) in the devcontainer.

## Releasing

When you are ready to publish a release to npm.

1. Make sure your git working dir is clean
2. Determine what version you will use
   > Currently during the beta period we have arbitrarily chosen
as the base version, with a

suffix.
   >
   > So if the current version is
> That script will update version numbers in

files, tag, and push to github.
   >
   > ️🚨 if the version script fails, run

4. Go to the [New Release](https://github.com/editframe/elements/releases/new) page. From there, select the tag related to the version you just pushed. Give the release a title and describe the contents of the release. When you "Publish" the release, an automated github action will build and release the tagged version.
